name: neural-weights-en-gb
$comment_661: 6.6.1 (2026-07-24) is an ARTIFACT + DECODE bundle revision on an UNCHANGED model: same v385 encoder bytes as 6.6.0 (see base_model), pair-index-gb.bin re-cut at δ=10 (was 5.0 — calibrated for a candidate checkpoint that was never promoted, provably inert on the shipped model: 0/69), consumed by @mailwoman/neural ≥7.7.0's probe CHAIN (probeMode "auto": segment path on comma'd input, anchored-adjacent path on comma-free). The 6.7.0 staged-candidate lineage referenced by 6.6.0's card is CLOSED (stop rule, five falsified training mechanisms — see the base card's phase); this capability is carried entirely by the decode-time prior on the shipped model.
$comment_new_card: Card became overlay-LOCAL in the 7.6.0 cycle (previously the #1177 card-less-overlay fallback to the base card). `labels`/`requires`/`architecture`/`components_supported` are duplicated VERBATIM from the base card so decode behavior matches the base exactly — keep in lockstep on every base ship.
model_lineage: Data-only overlay. Ships NO model.onnx/tokenizer.model of its own — resolves both from `@mailwoman/neural-weights-en-us` via `mailwoman.baseWeights` (byte-identical artifact; one encoder serves both locales). What this package ships: `postcode-gb.bin` (GB outward-code postcode-anchor binary, built from the WOF GB postcode shard) and `pair-index-gb.bin` (PIX1 placetype-pair retrieval index, built from HM Land Registry PPD tuples, δ=10 as of 6.6.1).
phase: SHIPPED (6.6.1, 2026-07-24) — GB dependent_locality goes LIVE via the decode-time placetype-pair prior on the untouched shipped v385 base. The Option-A δ-sweep (task-8 report, 2026-07-23) showed v385's dead-classifier deficit for the tag is large but UNIFORM (~7.0 logits mean), so δ=10 clears the entire 69-row golden board at once: 69/69 emit, 69/69 tag-correct, 0/51 golden false positives — exceeding every trained candidate the resurrection arc produced. Comma-free coverage comes from @mailwoman/neural 7.7.0's anchored-adjacent probe path (50/69 emit, 94% tag-correct, venue floor 0.062%). The training-side resurrection lineage (v3.10–v3.13) is CLOSED: five mechanisms falsified, stop rule executed three times; model-side redesign is unhurried research, not this capability's dependency.

base_model.$comment: Same model BYTES as 6.6.0 (v385, model.onnx md5 700f3cf4c08f31536f0bc053c281b19e) — the bundle version moves for the artifact re-cut, the encoder does not.
base_model.resolution: mailwoman.baseWeights in package.json — resolveWeights/resolveFromPackageDir resolve model.onnx + tokenizer.model from the base package dir (source suffixed "+base"); this card's own labels/requires/architecture below are a verbatim copy of the base's, not re-derived at runtime — keep them in lockstep on every base ship (same discipline as neural-weights-en-gb/scripts/link-dev-weights.ts's DEFAULT_MODEL/DEFAULT_TOKENIZER md5 lockstep comment).

postcode_gb_bin.granularity: outward code only (e.g. "SW1A", not the full unit "SW1A 1AA") — 2.7M unit postcodes is both too large for the browser budget and finer-grained than an anchor needs; a full unit code that misses the exact lookup falls back to its outward centroid automatically (extractPostcodeAnchors)
postcode_gb_bin.not_committed: derived artifact, .gitignore'd, materialized by scripts/copy-weights.ts at release time and scripts/link-dev-weights.ts for local dev — same posture as postcode-fr.bin

pair_index_gb_bin.format: PIX1 placetype-pair index (placetype-pair-prior arc, Task 3) — a retrieval-augmented (child, parent) dependent_locality prior, e.g. ("Fishburn", "Stockton-on-Tees")
pair_index_gb_bin.source: HM Land Registry Price Paid Data (PPD) tuples CSV, $MAILWOMAN_DATA_ROOT/ppd/2026-07-22/gb-tuples.csv, md5 dbd675bdc970ea60f96f0a470429a128, 25,674,048 rows
pair_index_gb_bin.delta_calibration: δ=10 (Option-A sweep on the SHIPPED v385, task-8 report 2026-07-23 — supersedes 6.6.0's δ=5.0, which was calibrated for the never-promoted feed-8k candidate and is provably inert on v385: 0/69). v385's dead-row deficit is uniform (~7.0 logits mean, 4.95 min), so the emit/correct curves and the venue-FP curve all saturate together at δ=10 (δ=12/15 byte-flat). δ remains a recall/FP dial in general — the sweep table lives in eval.delta_sweep_v385 below.
pair_index_gb_bin.probe_mode: "auto" (@mailwoman/neural ≥7.7.0 default): the segment path on comma'd input (whole comma-delimited segments — byte-identical to the previous "segment" default by construction AND re-measurement), the ANCHORED-ADJACENT path on comma-free input (parent window anchored immediately left of the postcode-shaped span or string-final; child window immediately adjacent, left-maximal; bias on the child span only). Window mode (any-to-any geometry) remains opt-in-only: 79% venue-confound FP at δ=10 — the geometry, not the probing, is its flaw (see the 2026-07-24 brainstorm-response doc).
pair_index_gb_bin.country_gate: hard-gated — the prior only fires when the resolved locale's country matches the index header's country (gb). Never crosses into en-us parses (see neural/test/weights.test.ts).
pair_index_gb_bin.not_committed: derived artifact, .gitignore'd (same posture as postcode-gb.bin) — rebuilt via `mailwoman gazetteer pair-index --out neural-weights-en-gb --country gb --source <ppd-csv> --delta 10`
pair_index_gb_bin.$comment_663: 6.6.3 re-cut: header gains transitionBeta 5 — on a pair hit, a position-scoped bonus on the transition into B-dependent_locality at the child's first piece (neural ≥7.8.1). Fixes the comma-free path-fusion class: 55→63/69 emit, 95.2% tag-correct. Title-preposition suppression ({at, of} predecessors) withholds the bonus inside lexicalized venue names (the 'New Inn at Hoff' class) — venue-anchored FP restored to the exact pre-beta 4/6500. Rebuild: gazetteer pair-index --delta 10 --transition-beta 5.

architecture.$comment: Verbatim copy of the base card's architecture — same encoder, no GB-specific change.

requires.$comment: Copy of the base card's requires block — same trained ship-config, same fail-closed contract via createScorer. Keep in lockstep with neural-weights-en-us/model-card.json on every base ship, EXCEPT conventions.mode, which is deliberately locale-pinned (see conventions.$comment).

conventions.$comment: PINNED to gb, not auto (#1275, 2026-07-24): auto reads the locale head, and the head under-detects GB on this overlay (US=0.780 below the 0.8 bar on the comma'd repro row, US=0.986 comma-free) — so the codex gb conventions row (the postcode snap-repair gate) was unreachable and the clip class (SK11 9PD → 'K11 9PD') stood at 44/106 on the gb-golden postcode rows. This bundle exists solely for GB parses, so the system is known a priori — pinning is the ship-default the diagnosis prescribed. Measured: exact 26 → 83, clips 44 → 0.

notes: 6.6.1 is the release where GB dependent_locality actually WORKS on what ships: the prior alone on the untouched v385 base. History for readers of 6.6.0's card: that card's numbers (69/69 @ δ=5 etc.) were measured on a staged candidate checkpoint (feed-8k) that failed the Gauntlet's metamorphic layer and was never promoted — on the SHIPPED model, δ=5 did nothing (0/69). The Option-A sweep found v385's deficit uniform and δ=10 sufficient; the anchored-adjacent probe path (neural 7.7.0) then added comma-free coverage at a 0.062% venue floor, superseding the comma-scoped-v1 framing before it shipped. The two comma'd-board misses of earlier cards are gone (69/69 tag-correct at δ=10); the comma-free board's 19 misses are 17 Viterbi path-level fusions (bias wins per-token, the global path routes through a fused street/locality run — a model-side ceiling the prior cannot cross) + 2 non-adjacent-geometry rows. Venue-confound floors documented honestly: 3.338% on the comma'd board (segment path, 217/6500 — the street-verbatim-child residual), 0.062% on the comma-free board (anchored path, 4/6500). All numbers: .superpowers/sdd/task-8-report.md §§ 'Option A', 'Ship battery', 'Experiment 0', 'v1.1 anchored-mode battery'; design record: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-24-pair-prior-comma-scope-{KIMI_HANDOFF,BRAINSTORM_RESPONSE}.md.

eval.$comment: All rows measured on the SHIPPED v385 base + this package's δ=10 pair-index via @mailwoman/neural 7.7.0's auto probe chain (2026-07-24 battery, pre-registered bars).

gb_golden_comma_stripped.note: transition-beta (6.6.3): 8 net-new recoveries over the anchor-channel baseline; residual 6 rows named in the task-8 report (2 multi-piece-continuation, unreachable at any β).

window_mode_reference.note: opt-in-only mode, documented for contrast — the any-to-any pair geometry is the flaw

delta_sweep_v385.$comment: Option-A sweep, segment mode, comma'd gb-golden board (emit/tag-correct of 69) + venue-confound FP%.

eval.invariance_suite: zero NEW violation classes vs the v385 baseline with the auto chain active; 138 INVARIANT pairs vs segment-only's 134 (the chain improves the profile).
eval.presets_and_golden: 6 demo presets + golden us/fr byte-identical to v385 (0/4,206 rows differ) — the en-gb country gate + the chain's comma'd/comma-free dispatch leave non-GB parses untouched by construction and by measurement.
eval.gauntlet: the base model is the SHIPPED v385 — the gauntlet's shipped-baseline status is unchanged by this data-only bundle.

format.$comment: Model/tokenizer format is the base's — see neural-weights-en-us/model-card.json. This overlay's own artifacts:

$comment_662: 6.6.2 (2026-07-24): version lockstep with the base bundle (en-nz overlay joins; the (x,x) identity-pair segment rule lands in neural 7.8.0 — GB is structurally inert to it, 0 identity pairs in this index, re-measured exact). GB artifacts and eval numbers unchanged from 6.6.1.
